name: Linear
tagline: Issue tracking and project management for high-performance teams.
description: Linear is a streamlined issue tracking and project management platform designed specifically for software teams that value speed and efficiency. With its keyboard-first interface and powerful automation engine, Linear helps engineering teams plan, track, and ship software faster while reducing administrative overhead.
pros: Blazing fast performance with instant search and filtering
Excellent keyboard shortcuts for power users and developers
Clean, intuitive UI with minimal learning curve for new users
Powerful AI integration for issue triage and backlog management
Deep GitHub and GitLab integration with branch-picking workflows
cons: Limited customization compared to more mature project management tools
No native time tracking or resource management features
Free tier is restrictive for larger teams and extended use

features.name: Issue Tracking
features.description: Create, assign, and track issues with rich markdown descriptions and attachments

features.name: Project Roadmaps
features.description: Visual roadmap views to plan and communicate project timelines

features.name: Sprint Planning
features.description: Cycle-based sprint management with automated progress tracking

features.name: AI Issue Triage
features.description: Automatic issue labeling, prioritization, and assignment using AI

features.name: GitHub Integration
features.description: Link issues to PRs, branches, and commits with automatic status updates

features.name: GitLab Integration
features.description: Full GitLab support including merge request linking and branch creation

features.name: Slack Integration
features.description: Create and update issues directly from Slack with rich notifications

features.name: Figma Integration
features.description: Embed Figma designs and prototypes directly in Linear issues

features.name: API & GraphQL
features.description: Powerful GraphQL API for custom integrations and automations

features.name: Keyboard Shortcuts
features.description: Comprehensive keyboard-driven workflow for maximum efficiency

features.name: Views & Filters
features.description: Custom views with advanced filtering, sorting, and saved searches

features.name: Cycle Analytics
features.description: Team velocity tracking and cycle metrics for continuous improvement

features.name: Role-Based Access
features.description: Admin, member, and viewer roles with granular project permissions

features.name: Audit Logs
features.description: Detailed audit trail of all changes and actions across the workspace

features.name: Mobile App
features.description: Native iOS and Android apps for issue management on the go

features.name: Push Notifications
features.description: Real-time notifications for mentions, assignments, and status changes

features.name: Import Tools
features.description: Bulk import from Jira, GitHub Issues, Asana, and Trello

features.name: Automation Rules
features.description: Custom workflow automations for issue status changes and assignments

content.title: Overview
content.body: Linear has emerged as the go-to issue tracking tool for modern software teams that prioritize speed and developer experience. Built from the ground up with a keyboard-first philosophy, Linear minimizes context switching and allows engineers to manage their work without leaving their flow state. Its clean, minimal interface belies a powerful automation engine that helps teams triage, prioritize, and ship features faster.

content.title: Who is it for?
content.body: Linear is designed for engineering teams and technical organizations that value fast, keyboard-driven workflows. It excels in startup and mid-growth environments where speed and efficiency are critical. Product teams working closely with engineering will also appreciate Linear's roadmap and cycle planning features, though non-technical users may find the interface too minimalist.

content.title: Key Features
content.body: Linear shines in its speed and developer-centric design. The keyboard-first interface allows users to navigate, create, and update issues without touching a mouse. Its AI-powered issue triage automatically labels and prioritizes incoming work, while deep GitHub and GitLab integrations keep code and tasks in sync. The cycle analytics feature provides actionable insights for team retrospectives.
items: Keyboard-first interface with hundreds of customizable shortcuts for power users
AI-driven issue triage that automatically labels, prioritizes, and assigns incoming issues
Deep GitHub and GitLab integration with automatic branch and PR linking
Cycle-based sprint planning with velocity tracking and burndown charts
Powerful GraphQL API enabling custom tooling and advanced automations

content.title: Who should avoid it?
content.body: Teams that need extensive customization, custom fields, or complex workflow configurations may find Linear too opinionated and restrictive. Organizations that rely on Gantt charts, resource management, or time tracking will need to supplement Linear with other tools. Non-technical teams or those accustomed to more traditional project management interfaces may struggle with Linear's minimalist, keyboard-driven approach.

faqs.question: What is Linear best used for?
faqs.answer: Linear is best used for software project management and issue tracking in engineering teams. It excels at managing sprints, tracking bugs and feature requests, and planning product roadmaps. Its deep version control integrations make it particularly valuable for development teams that use GitHub or GitLab.

faqs.question: How much does Linear cost?
faqs.answer: Linear offers a free tier limited to 10 users with basic features. The Team plan starts at $8/month per user and includes unlimited projects, cycles, and integrations. The Enterprise plan at $16/month per user adds SAML SSO, audit logs, and dedicated support with a 99.95% uptime SLA.

faqs.question: Does Linear integrate with GitHub?
faqs.answer: Yes, Linear offers deep GitHub integration that allows teams to link issues to branches, pull requests, and commits. When a PR is merged, the linked Linear issue can automatically transition to the Done state, keeping development workflows synchronized without manual updates.

faqs.question: Is Linear suitable for large teams?
faqs.answer: Yes, Linear supports large teams with its Enterprise plan that includes SAML SSO, advanced audit logging, and dedicated support. However, teams that require extensive customization or non-engineering workflows may find Linear's focused scope limiting compared to more general-purpose tools.

author: StackPilot Team
